title: notifications core — broadcast channels
description: Real-time channels for the inbox UX. The channel binding lives in this module; notifications-in-app is the module that actually pushes NotificationCreated messages onto these channels. Uses Laravel Reverb (self-hosted WebSocket server) in dev + prod; Pusher/Ably remain as pluggable alternatives via BroadcastManager.

channels.name: user.{id}.notifications
channels.purpose: Per-user private inbox live-updates. New in-app notifications, seen-count changes, and archive events push here. Client subscribes on inbox mount + updates the bell badge in real time.
eventsPushed: notifications.archived — archived by user or system

channels.name: tenant.{id}.notifications
channels.purpose: Tenant-admin realtime dashboard. Aggregate send counts, delivery-failure spikes, template publish events. Used by the admin sends dashboard SDUI screen.
eventsPushed: notifications.tenant.failed — delivery failure signal for the dashboard alert widget

notes: Broadcast events are additive to the events in events.json. events.json fires domain events on the Laravel event dispatcher; broadcast events push serialised payloads onto WebSocket channels. Not every domain event is broadcast — only the ones with live-UI value.
In-app notification content itself is delivered by notifications-in-app writing to the database + immediately pushing a `notifications.created` broadcast so the bell updates without a page refresh.
